one_line_takeaway: DPO-driven alignment collapses response diversity so severely (40% single-cluster on TruthfulQA under Jaccard, 79% under embedding clustering) that all sampling-based uncertainty-quantification methods (semantic entropy, SelfCheckGPT, even NLI-based and SINdex variants) become structurally uninformative on affected queries, while free per-token entropy from a single greedy pass retains signal because DPO preserves 66% of token-level entropy even as it destroys output-level diversity.
key_finding: On the questions where an aligned model collapses to a single semantic cluster across 10 samples (40.0% of TruthfulQA under Jaccard; 79.0% under embedding clustering), every sampling-based uncertainty method tested -- semantic entropy (Jaccard-SE, Embedding-SE), SelfCheckGPT, canonical NLI-based SE at three DeBERTa scales (all ~0.50-0.51 AUROC, with 6.2x model scaling from 70M to 435M yielding zero AUROC improvement), and even the full SINdex metric with intra-cluster coherence weighting (AUROC=0.451, actually below plain SE-Embedding's 0.509) -- has essentially zero discriminative power, because they measure inter-sample diversity that DPO has structurally removed. Free per-token entropy (B1), computed on a single greedy pass, retains signal (AUROC=0.603 on single-cluster questions, 0.599 overall on TruthfulQA vs LLM-judge labels) because it measures per-token computational uncertainty, which DPO training preserves at 66% of its base-model magnitude (1.175->0.776 nats) even as it collapses output-level diversity to a single mode. The effect is causal (base-vs-instruct ablation, p<1e-6), localized to the DPO stage rather than SFT (two independent chains), persists across sample sizes (N=3-10), temperatures (T=0.3-1.5, 62%->38% SCR), decoding strategies, and generation lengths (40-200 tokens, 33.5% SCR persists at 200 tokens vs 0% for base), and varies by roughly 50x in severity across model families/recipes (Tulu-3's DPO+RLVR: 0.5% SCR vs Qwen3-14B: 28.5% SCR).
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution (not related work) is: (1) a diagnostic finding termed the 'alignment tax' — on TruthfulQA (n=790), 40.0% of questions collapse to a single semantic cluster (SCR, single-cluster rate) across N=10 i.i.d. samples at T=1.0 under the paper's primary metric, Jaccard bigram clustering (threshold=0.4), and 79.0% under an embedding-cosine sensitivity clustering; on such single-cluster questions, sampling-based uncertainty methods (semantic entropy, SelfCheckGPT-style) have AUROC=0.500 (zero discriminative power) while free per-token entropy (called 'B1') retains AUROC=0.603; (2) causal localization via a base-vs-instruct ablation on Qwen3-14B (1.0% base SCR vs 28.5% instruct SCR, Wilcoxon p<1e-6) and two independent training-stage chains (Mistral/Zephyr: Base 0.0% -> SFT 1.5% -> DPO 4.0% SCR; Llama/Tulu-3: Base 0.0% -> SFT 0.0% -> DPO+RLVR 0.5% SCR) showing DPO, not SFT, drives the collapse, with severity being recipe- and family-dependent (0.5%-28.5% SCR across families/recipes); (3) a stage-wise token-entropy measurement (Base 1.175 -> SFT 1.055 -> DPO 0.776 nats, p=1.17e-40) showing DPO retains 66% of per-token entropy while collapsing response-level diversity, explaining the entropy/diversity decoupling; (4) demonstration that the tax is task-dependent (GSM8K token entropy AUROC=0.724, Cohen's d=0.81, vs TruthfulQA AUROC~0.52-0.60, d=0.07); and (5) an exploratory architectural artifact, the UCBD cascade (five 'cognitive boundaries' B1-B5 ordered cheapest-first), motivated by the diagnostic finding, achieving 57% cost savings under weak inter-boundary dependence (|r|<=0.12) and raising GSM8K selective-prediction accuracy from 84.4% to 93.2% at 50% coverage.
threat_model: None. This is not a security/adversarial paper and the authors do not propose one. There is no attacker, no adversarial objective, and no attack surface analyzed. The paper is an empirical ML/NLP diagnostic study of how RLHF/DPO alignment training affects response diversity and its downstream effect on uncertainty-quantification (UQ) methods. It does briefly reference (in Related Work, not as its own contribution) a third-party audit of moderation-induced homogenization from safety filters (Stanusch et al., 2025) and notes this is a distinct, complementary phenomenon to the training-time effect the paper measures -- but that moderation/security angle is explicitly attributed to other authors' work, not analyzed by this paper.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) the highest-SCR family (Qwen3-14B) lacks a public SFT-only checkpoint, so DPO attribution there is inferential rather than a direct stage-wise measurement (though supported by base-vs-instruct + consistent SFT pattern on the two chains that do have checkpoints); (2) all experiments use 4-bit quantized models -- cross-quantization checks (Q4 vs Q8 on Mistral-7B) show identical SCR at semantic thresholds, but FP16 runs are left for future work; (3) LLM-judge correctness labels (LLaMA-3.2-3B) show only moderate agreement with TruthfulQA gold templates (Cohen's kappa=0.487), though the core SCR finding is explicitly label-free; (4) refusal filtering was not needed for the open-source models tested but could inflate SCR differently on commercial models with aggressive safety filters (a distinct, moderation-induced homogenization source the paper does not measure); (5) B5 (NLI grounding) validation uses gold reference answers unavailable at inference time, so a production deployment would likely see lower AUC; (6) the Pointer/router model is evaluated only via 5-fold CV on TruthfulQA, with held-out/cross-domain generalization untested; (7) on GSM8K, response length (AUROC=0.849) dominates and subsumes the incremental value of entropy (0.724, r=0.53 with length) for selective prediction -- entropy's claimed value is cross-task generality (useful on TruthfulQA where length is uninformative), not a GSM8K-specific gain; (8) generalization to closed-source/GPT-class models and non-QA domains (code, dialogue) is unconfirmed; scope is limited to 3B-14B open-weight models.

datasets_used: TruthfulQA (790 questions full set; 200q first-n subset for ablations; 401q subset for independence experiments)
FreshQA (3x500 questions per model from a 600-question pool; time-sensitive QA with freshness categories)
quantitative_results: TruthfulQA single-cluster rate (SCR): 40.0% under Jaccard bigram clustering (n=790), 79.0% under embedding-cosine clustering
On single-cluster questions: sampling-based semantic entropy AUROC=0.500 (chance); B1 token entropy AUROC=0.603

Overall TruthfulQA (LLM-judge labels): B1 mean entropy AUROC=0.599 vs SelfCheck-Emb 0.588, SE-Jaccard 0.548, SE-Embedding 0.542, SE-NLI (DeBERTa-large/base/xsmall) 0.511/0.512/0.501, full SINdex 0.451
GSM8K (n=500): B1 token entropy AUROC=0.706-0.724 (Cohen's d=0.81); response length alone AUROC=0.849; combined length+entropy 0.844
GSM8K selective prediction: accuracy 84.4% baseline -> 93.2% at 50% coverage (entropy gate); 92.0%/88.7% at 30%/80% coverage
Base-vs-instruct ablation (Qwen3-14B, n=200): SCR 1.0% (base) vs 28.5% (instruct), mean clusters 9.26 vs 3.58, Wilcoxon p<1e-6
Training-stage ablation, Mistral/Zephyr chain (n=200): SCR Base 0.0% -> SFT 1.5% -> SFT+DPO 4.0%, all pairwise p<0.003
Training-stage ablation, Llama/Tulu-3 chain (n=200): SCR Base 0.0% -> SFT 0.0% -> SFT+DPO+RLVR 0.5%
Cross-family SCR (n=200): Qwen3-14B-Instruct 28.5%, LLaMA-3.2-3B-Instruct 5.5%, Mistral-7B-Instruct 1.0%, Qwen3-14B-Base 1.0%
Stage-wise per-token entropy (Zephyr chain): Base 1.175 -> SFT 1.055 -> DPO 0.776 nats (p=1.17e-40); DPO retains 66% of token-level entropy
Temperature ablation (n=50, N=5): SCR 62% at T=0.3 down to 38% at T=1.5

Generation-length ablation (n=50): SCR 32.0% (40 tokens) -> 10.0% (100 tokens) -> 8.0% (200 tokens), vs ~0% for base model at all lengths; extended to n=200 at 200 tokens: SCR=33.5% under embedding tau=0.85 (down from 79.0% at 40 tokens)
Cross-embedder validation (n=50, tau=0.85): Qwen3-Embedding SCR=78.0% vs Nomic-embed-text SCR=92.0%
Quantization sensitivity (Mistral-7B, n=30): Q4 vs Q8 SCR identical at 6.7% (threshold=0.7)
Cascade cost savings: beta1=0.426 (57.4% of queries resolved at free B1 stage alone), Ccascade ~= 0.716 x Cparallel
SINdex full-metric replication (Exp 27, n=200): AUROC=0.451, below SE-Embedding's 0.509
LLM-judge vs TruthfulQA gold-template agreement: Cohen's kappa=0.487
B1-B2 independence: Pearson r=0.119, distance correlation=0.143, MI=0.008 bits (n=401)
baselines_compared: Semantic Entropy / SE-Jaccard and SE-Embedding (Kuhn et al. 2023 methodology, reimplemented)
Canonical NLI-based SE at three DeBERTa-v3 scales: xsmall (70M), base (184M), large (435M)
Full SINdex metric with intra-cluster coherence weighting (Abdaljalil et al. 2025, reimplemented by the authors)
Response length as a naive difficulty/uncertainty proxy on GSM8K
